At retirement, you don't withdraw savings - you activate your network, receiving micro-payments from each thread proportional to the connected entity's current productivity. Thread strength decays slowly; thread quantity compounds. Variable income creates variable thread patterns, which is fine - the network aggregates stochastically. Implementation requires: thread registry (blockchain or state-backed ledger), thread valuation oracle, and decay/activation protocols.",
  "what_is_new": "Replaces accumulation (balance) with connection (topology). Variable earners excel at building diverse economic relationships - this system leverages that existing skill rather than fighting their income pattern.",
  "why_it_works": "Single accounts are fragile (can be emptied, inflated away, mismanaged). A web of 10,000 small obligations is antifragile - individual thread failures don't collapse the network. The 'gnomic' cryptic-promise nature of threads removes 'am I saving enough?' anxiety - you participate, threads accumulate, the aggregate stabilizes.",

solution.label: Mycelial Obligation Threads
solution.design_principles: Inconsistency is not a bug but a feature. Replace the single vulnerable account with a dense web of micro-obligations. Retirement wealth is network topology, not balance.
solution.core_mechanism: Every economic transaction creates a bidirectional 'thread' - a micro-commitment between parties. Retirement is pulling on your accumulated thread network rather than withdrawing from an account.
solution.how_it_works: Each transaction (payment received, purchase made, service rendered) auto-generates a cryptographic 'thread' - a 0.1-0.5% fractional claim on future value from that counterparty (individual, business, or pooled collective). Threads are bidirectional: you owe them, they owe you. Over a career, you accumulate thousands of threads. At retirement, you don't withdraw savings - you activate your network, receiving micro-payments from each thread proportional to the connected entity's current productivity. Thread strength decays slowly; thread quantity compounds. Variable income creates variable thread patterns, which is fine - the network aggregates stochastically. Implementation requires: thread registry (blockchain or state-backed ledger), thread valuation oracle, and decay/activation protocols.
solution.what_is_new: Replaces accumulation (balance) with connection (topology). Variable earners excel at building diverse economic relationships - this system leverages that existing skill rather than fighting their income pattern.
solution.why_it_works: Single accounts are fragile (can be emptied, inflated away, mismanaged). A web of 10,000 small obligations is antifragile - individual thread failures don't collapse the network. Variable earners already think in transactions, not percentages; this matches their mental model. The 'gnomic' cryptic-promise nature of threads removes 'am I saving enough?' anxiety - you participate, threads accumulate, the aggregate stabilizes.
solution.why_it_fails: Enforcement across decades is genuinely hard - how do you make a defunct business or deceased individual honor a thread from 30 years ago? Requires either a robust legal framework for thread inheritance/succession or a massive pooled backstop that socializes thread defaults. Also creates perverse incentives: people might prefer many weak threads to fewer strong ones, gaming quantity over quality.
solution.medium_term: Thread networks become visible social infrastructure. Your 'retirement health' becomes a measure of economic connectedness rather than account balance. Variable workers might have better retirement prospects than salaried workers with fewer transaction relationships.
solution.long_term_vision: Retirement ceases to be a saved quantity and becomes an emergent property of lifetime economic participation. The distinction between 'retired' and 'working' blurs - you're always both accumulating and redeeming threads. Income volatility becomes irrelevant because the network itself is the shock absorber. Wealth inequality partially shifts from 'who has more money' to 'who has denser, healthier networks' - potentially more equalizing since network-building scales sublinearly with income.
